title: Update to pricing_version webhook field
excerpt:
Webhooks will now return the current pricing version ID in the pricing_version field for free plans. Previously, the field would return null. 
Check out the sample payload below, and you also can read more about webhooks…

posts.author: rachelatmonday (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Webhooks will now return the current pricing version ID in the pricing_version field for free plans. Previously, the field would return null.

Check out the sample payload below, and you also can read more about webhooks in our documentation!

  type: "install",
  data: {
    app_id: 1000000000,
    user_id: 2,
    user_email: "user1@users.com",
    user_name: "User 1",
    user_cluster: "other",
    account_tier: "free",
    account_max_users: 10000,
    account_id: 777777,
    account_name: "Demo Account",
    account_slug: "test",
    version_data: { major: 4, minor: 5, patch: 0, type: "minor" },
    timestamp: "2023-06-26T00:00:00.000+00:00",
    subscription: {
      plan_id: "5",
      renewal_date: "2023-07-10T00:00:00+00:00",
      is_trial: false,
      billing_period: "monthly",
      days_left: 14,
      pricing_version: 5
    },
    user_country: "IL"
  }

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml: Does this include the pricing_version in the JWT sent with integration events? Previously, I believe, free would send null there too.

posts.author: rachelatmonday (Rachel)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @anon29275264,

Apologies for the delay. Just confirmed with the team that they are unrelated. The announced change only impacted the pricing_version field for webhooks.

Best,
Rachel
